title: Review money transfer and currency routine (South Africa)
shortDescription: If you send or receive money from South Africa, review transfer costs and timing.
whyItMatters: Early banking and transfer planning can reduce first-month cash flow stress.
whatThisInvolves: Set a routine that works for you
Keep SA-issued records accessible if needed
typicalSteps: Check Wise, bank, or other options
Do a test transfer if needed

title: Check South African driving licence use in the Netherlands
shortDescription: Confirm how long you can drive on a SA licence and when you must exchange.
whyItMatters: Rules differ by country; knowing your deadline avoids last-minute stress.
whatThisInvolves: Check RDW rules for non-EU licences
whenToDoIt: Week 3–4 if you plan to drive.

title: Review which South African records to keep accessible
shortDescription: Keep SA-issued official records (e.g. birth, marriage) accessible for registration or family admin.
whyItMatters: Some municipality or IND steps may require originals or certified copies.
whatThisInvolves: Keep originals or apostilled copies safe
Know where to get new copies if needed

title: Review US banking and tax follow-up awareness
shortDescription: Note any US tax or banking follow-up you need (e.g. FBAR, reporting) during the transition.
whyItMatters: US persons have ongoing reporting obligations; early awareness avoids oversights.
whatThisInvolves: Keep US financial access stable if needed

title: Check US driving licence use in the Netherlands
shortDescription: Confirm how long you can drive on a US licence and when to exchange.
whyItMatters: Non-EU licence rules apply; know your deadline.
whenToDoIt: Week 3–4 if you plan to drive.

title: Keep US financial access stable during transition
shortDescription: Ensure you can still access US accounts and cards while setting up Dutch banking.
whyItMatters: Avoid being locked out of funds or credit during the move.
whatThisInvolves: Update US bank contact details if needed
Note any reporting you must do

title: Review money transfer setup and timing (India)

whyItMatters: Transfer costs and timing can affect monthly budgeting.
whatThisInvolves: Keep India-issued records accessible if needed

title: Check India driving licence use in the Netherlands
shortDescription: Confirm how long you can drive on an Indian licence and when to exchange.

title: Keep India-issued official records accessible if needed
shortDescription: Keep Indian official documents (e.g. birth, marriage) accessible for registration or admin.

title: Check UK driving licence use in the Netherlands
shortDescription: Confirm post-Brexit rules for UK licence use and exchange.
whyItMatters: UK licence rules changed; know your deadline to exchange.
whenToDoIt: Week 3–4 if you plan to drive.

title: Review UK to NL banking and subscription transition
shortDescription: Close or redirect UK accounts and subscriptions as you settle in the Netherlands.
whyItMatters: Clean transition reduces double payments and admin.
whatThisInvolves: List UK direct debits and subscriptions

title: Check Canada driving licence use in the Netherlands
shortDescription: Confirm how long you can drive on a Canadian licence and when to exchange.
whyItMatters: Non-EU licence rules apply; know your deadline.
whenToDoIt: Week 3–4 if you plan to drive.

title: Review Canada to NL banking transition
shortDescription: Plan transfer and account transition from Canada to the Netherlands.
whyItMatters: Smooth transition avoids cash flow gaps.
whatThisInvolves: Plan closure or retention of Canadian accounts

title: Check Australia driving licence use in the Netherlands

title: Review Australia to NL banking awareness
shortDescription: Plan transfer and account use from Australia to the Netherlands.

whatThisInvolves: Note any Australian tax or reporting

title: Check EU driving licence rules and renewal awareness
shortDescription: EU licence holders can often use their licence longer; check renewal and exchange rules.
whyItMatters: EU rules differ from non-EU; know when you must renew or exchange.
whatThisInvolves: Note renewal date of your licence
whenToDoIt: Week 3–4 if you plan to drive.

title: Stabilise cross-border bank or payment routine if relevant
shortDescription: If you keep an EU bank account, ensure payments and access work from the Netherlands.
whyItMatters: Cross-border access can simplify some payments or cause friction; clarify early.

title: Check UAE-issued driving licence rules in the Netherlands if you plan to drive
shortDescription: Confirm how long you can drive on your licence and when exchange may be required.
whyItMatters: Rules depend on issuing country and agreements; know your deadline if you need a car.
whatThisInvolves: Check RDW guidance for your licence origin
whenToDoIt: Week 3–4 if you plan to drive.

title: Review UAE to NL banking transition
shortDescription: Plan transfers from the UAE and stabilise Dutch account use after BSN.
whyItMatters: Smooth FX and account access avoids cash-flow stress after a long-haul move.
whatThisInvolves: Compare transfer options from AED to EUR
Align UAE account closure or retention with tax advice if needed
Update UAE banks with your new address where required

title: Keep UAE-issued official records accessible if needed
shortDescription: Retain attested UAE documents and translations if municipality or IND follow-up requests them.
whyItMatters: Follow-up appointments often reference the same civil or residence proofs you used for entry.
whatThisInvolves: Keep digital and physical copies of attested documents
Note translation dates and translator details
